title: carex assiniboinensis
intro: Carex assiniboinensis, also known as Assiniboine sedge, is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial herb native to North America, commonly found in wet meadows and along streams. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Carex assiniboinensis include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and acting as an expectorant to relieve congestion.
